_note: Synthetic BROKEN evidence, one per automated indicator, used to teach assessors what a real finding looks like. The Trust Center generator runs each row set through the real engine/checks.py and only attaches it if the check genuinely returns False, so no verdict here is hand-authored. Never mixed into the live posture count.

KSI-PIY-GIV.why: The authoritative inventory query returned zero resources, so an on-demand inventory cannot be demonstrated. The check requires at least one row.

KSI-CNA-MAT.why: A publicly-exposed resource is present. The check passes only when the exposure query returns nothing; here it returns a public IP attachment.

KSI-IAM-SNU.why: No managed identity was found for secretless workload authentication. The check requires at least one.

KSI-MLA-OSM.why: No Sentinel-onboarded Log Analytics workspace was found, so a SIEM capability cannot be evidenced.

KSI-SVC-SIN.why: A storage account still permits TLS 1.0 and non-HTTPS traffic. The check requires every account to enforce TLS1_2 and HTTPS-only.

KSI-SVC-ASM.why: A Key Vault has purge protection off and public network access enabled. The check requires purge protection on and public access Disabled for every vault.

KSI-CNA-RNT.why: A network security group carries no inbound Deny rule, so it has no explicit default-deny. The check requires at least one inbound Deny per NSG.

KSI-CNA-ULN.why: A virtual network has only one subnet, so it provides no real segmentation. The check requires at least two subnets.

KSI-SVC-VCM.why: No private endpoints were found validating service-to-service communication. The check requires at least one.

KSI-RPL-ABO.why: No backup policy is defined in the vault, so backups cannot be aligned to recovery objectives. The check requires at least one policy.

KSI-CNA-DFP.why: No policy assignment governs the resource group, so functionality and privileges are not being constrained. The check requires at least one assignment.

KSI-IAM-ELP.why: A role assignment grants Owner at the resource group scope, which violates least privilege. The check fails if any assignment resolves to the built-in Owner role.

KSI-IAM-APM.why: The only Conditional Access policy requiring MFA is disabled, so nothing is enforced. The check requires at least one ENABLED policy with MFA or an authentication strength.

KSI-IAM-SUS.why: No enabled risk-based Conditional Access policy responds to suspicious sign-ins. The check requires an enabled policy keyed on sign-in or user risk.

KSI-IAM-JIT.why: No PIM-eligible role assignments were found; privileged roles are standing rather than just-in-time. The check requires at least one eligible assignment.

KSI-SVC-EIS.why: No Defender secure score is being computed, so the evaluate-and-improve loop cannot be evidenced. The check requires at least one score.

KSI-CNA-IBP.why: Unhealthy best-practice findings outnumber healthy ones. The check requires healthy assessments to outnumber unhealthy.

KSI-CNA-EIS.why: Every policy assignment is audit-only (DoNotEnforce), so intended state is observed but never enforced. The check requires at least one assignment in Default (enforced) mode.

KSI-CMT-VTD.why: Automated validation fails more often than it passes, so tests are not reliably gating promotion. The check requires successes to outnumber failures.

KSI-CMT-RMV.why: No successful version-controlled redeployment was found, so changes cannot be traced to a pipeline run. The check requires at least one successful push-triggered run.

KSI-SCR-MON.why: An open dependency alert is rated critical. The check fails when any monitored alert is high or critical severity.

KSI-PIY-RSD.why: No static code-scanning analyses were found in the SDLC. The check requires at least one analysis run.

KSI-CMT-LMC.why: The diagnostic category is enabled but has shipped zero events to Log Analytics, so change logging cannot be evidenced. The check requires at least one category actively ingesting events.
